original_abstract: This article provides a comprehensive review of the current state-of-the-art in overlapping community detection, focusing on algorithms, quality metrics, and benchmarking standards. We conduct a rigorous comparative analysis of fourteen prominent algorithms. Beyond traditional community-level evaluations, we introduce a novel framework designed to assess an algorithm's precision in identifying overlapping nodes, specifically accounting for issues of overdetection and underdetection. By integrating community-level performance—measured via normalized mutual information and the Omega index—with node-level accuracy measured by the F-score, several key findings emerge. In networks with low overlapping density, SLPA, OSLOM, Game, and COPRA consistently outperform their competitors. In contrast, for networks characterized by high overlapping density and diversity, SLPA and Game demonstrate the most stable performance; however, our results indicate that accurately resolving structures in such complex networks remains an open challenge. Finally, our analysis of real-world networks reveals a consistent structural pattern: overlapping nodes typically constitute a small fraction of the network (generally under 30%) and rarely membership in more than two or three communities.
humanized_abstract: Community detection is a widely used technique in networks analysis. In this paper, we give an overview of the state of the art of overlapping community detection, focusing on algorithms, measures and benchmarks. We made a comparison between 14 algorithms. The last part is dedicated to the evaluation of the precision of an algorithm in detecting overlapping nodes, which corresponds to solving the overdetection and underdetection problems. To this end, we propose a new framework called FOC combining community level evaluation metrics such as normalized mutual information and Omega index with node level evaluation measures such as the F-score. This allows us to determine that, for low overlapping density networks, the best algorithms are SLPA, OSLOM, Game and COPRA. For high overlapping density networks and diversity, SLPA and Game are the most stable algorithms, but the resolution of the community structures in such cases is still an open problem. Finally, we evaluate the overlapping structure of real world graphs, and we show that they all have the same common characteristic: the overlapping nodes are always limited, i.e. less than 30% of the nodes, and the number of communities to which they belong is always limited, i.e. less than 3 or 4.
